title: 30/60/90 Onboarding Program Implementation Plan
content:
Phase 1: Audit (Weeks 1-2)

Interview the 3 most recent hires about their onboarding experience. Document the top 5 pain points and the undocumented systems they struggled with most.

Create a knowledge audit template and assign each team to document their top 3 tribal knowledge items. Focus on: deployment procedures, testing workflows, and architecture decisions.

Phase 2: Build (Weeks 3-4)

Design a structured 30/60/90 day onboarding program:
- Day 1-30: Environment setup guide (automated), architecture overview, first bug fix
- Day 31-60: Paired feature work, on-call shadow, system deep-dives
- Day 61-90: Independent feature delivery, knowledge contribution, mentor rotation

Create a mentor pairing rotation schedule. Each new hire gets a dedicated mentor for their first 90 days, rotating between 2-3 mentors to broaden exposure.

Phase 3: Pilot & Measure (Weeks 5-8)

Run the next hire through the program. Track:
- Time to first PR merged
- Time to first independent feature shipped
- Number of questions asked in Slack vs. answered by docs
- New hire satisfaction score at 30/60/90 days

Baseline: current average is 3+ months to independence. Target: under 6 weeks.
next_steps: Schedule knowledge audit kickoff with each team lead
Draft the 30/60/90 curriculum outline for review
Set up tracking dashboard for onboarding metrics

title: Weekly Knowledge Capture Ritual Recommendation
content:
Recommendation: Implement a weekly 'Knowledge Drop' ritual

Each Friday, one team member spends 30 minutes documenting a critical system, process, or decision that currently lives only in their head. The output is a short document (1-2 pages) added to a central knowledge base.

Why this works:
- Low time investment (30 min/week/person on rotation)
- Creates a growing library of institutional knowledge
- Distributes the documentation burden across the team
- Makes knowledge transfer continuous rather than crisis-driven

Implementation:
- Create a shared Notion/Confluence space with a template
- Set up a weekly Slack reminder with a random team member tagged
- Track completion rate and document quality in sprint retros
- After 3 months, audit: how many single-person dependencies remain?

Expected outcome: Within one quarter, reduce single-person knowledge dependencies by 50%. When someone goes on vacation or leaves, the team can continue operating without a multi-week knowledge transfer.
next_steps: Create the Knowledge Drop document template
Set up the Slack reminder bot with team rotation
Present the ritual at next all-hands for buy-in

title: CI/CD Pipeline Reliability Improvement Plan
content:
Problem: Deploy pipeline fails on 60% of attempts. Engineering spends ~4 hours/week debugging deploys.

Root Cause Analysis:
1. Flaky integration tests (primary) — 3 specific test suites intermittently fail due to timing issues and shared test state
2. Docker build cache invalidation — builds randomly pull stale layers
3. No deployment health checks — failures only detected after traffic is routed

Phase 1: Quick Wins (Week 1)
- Quarantine the 3 flakiest test suites (run them but don't block deploys)
- Add retry logic to Docker builds with cache bust on failure
- Estimated improvement: 60% fail rate → 30%

Phase 2: Test Infrastructure (Weeks 2-3)
- Rewrite quarantined tests with isolated state (no shared databases between tests)
- Add test timing budgets — any test taking >10s gets flagged for review
- Set up parallel test execution to reduce pipeline time from 25min to 10min

Phase 3: Deploy Safety (Weeks 3-4)
- Implement canary deploys (route 5% traffic first, monitor for 5min)
- Add automated rollback on error rate spike (>1% 5xx responses)
- Create deploy runbook for manual intervention

Success Metric: Deploy success rate >95% sustained for 2 weeks.
next_steps: Identify and tag the 3 flakiest test suites this week
Set up deploy success rate monitoring dashboard
Write canary deploy RFC for team review

title: Incident Response Runbook: Database Outages
content:
Incident Response Runbook — Database Outages

Severity: P0/P1
Owner: On-call engineer + Database team lead
Escalation: If not resolved in 30 minutes, page VP Engineering

Step 1: Triage (0-5 minutes)
- Check database dashboard: CPU, connections, replication lag
- Check recent deploys: was anything shipped in the last 2 hours?
- Check for known issues: is this a recurring pattern?

Step 2: Immediate Mitigation (5-15 minutes)
- If CPU >90%: identify and kill long-running queries (query log)
- If connections exhausted: restart connection pooler (PgBouncer)
- If replication lag >30s: pause non-critical read traffic, investigate primary

Step 3: Communication (parallel)
- Post in #incidents with: what's happening, who's investigating, ETA
- Update status page if customer-facing impact
- Notify affected team leads

Step 4: Resolution
- Apply fix (query optimization, config change, rollback deploy)
- Monitor for 15 minutes to confirm stability
- Update #incidents with resolution

Step 5: Post-Incident
- Write incident report within 24 hours
- Schedule blameless post-mortem within 48 hours
- Create tickets for follow-up actions
- Update this runbook if the incident revealed a gap
next_steps: Review runbook with database team for accuracy
Add runbook link to PagerDuty escalation policy
Schedule a tabletop exercise to practice the runbook
Create runbooks for the other top 4 failure modes
